item.title: Multi-backlog support — run sprints across multiple backlog files in one session
item.intent: aishore reads from all declared backlog files in a single session; items are picked by priority across all files with no manual pre-sorting required.
item.description: Users working on large projects split work across component-specific backlog files. Currently aishore only reads from backlog.json and bugs.json. Multi-backlog support lets users declare additional backlog files (e.g. backlog/infra.json, backlog/api.json) in config.yaml, and the auto and run commands pick items from all declared files, respecting priority ordering across files. This removes the need to manually shuttle items between files before running sprints.
steps: Add a backlog_files config key (list of paths) to config.yaml, defaulting to the current two-file set
Update all backlog-reading functions (pick_item, list_pickable_ids, count_by_status, etc.) to iterate over the configured file list
Update resolve_backlog_file to search all declared files when resolving an item ID
Validate declared paths exist and are readable at startup; warn on missing files, don't abort
Update cmd_status to show counts per declared file
Verify: run auto done across two backlog files with items in each — both are picked and completed
acceptanceCriteria: config.yaml accepts a backlog_files list; omitting it defaults to current behaviour
Items are picked from all declared files in priority order across files
backlog list shows items from all declared files
resolve_backlog_file finds items in any declared file
Missing declared files produce a warning, not an abort
